action.reasoning: Observation: Iter11 trained NME for 30 epochs/task and got val accuracy 0.1776, far below the iter5 linear classifier's 0.4655. NME is underperforming drastically, likely due to poor feature separation for cosine similarity. The previous softmax approach achieved 0.4655. Analysis: To maximize score quickly, we should revert to the linear classifier strategy and eliminate forgetting by storing all previously seen data (full replay) instead of a limited exemplar set. Since no memory constraint is imposed, we can accumulate the entire training set and train a joint model after each task. This should yield much higher accuracy. Decision: Rewrite train.py to maintain a full replay buffer (store all images/labels from all tasks so far) and train a softmax classifier with cross-entropy only (no distillation). Use the same ResNet-18 backbone with expanding linear head. Rewrite predict.py to use the model's classifier head. Run a quick training with 10 epochs/task to verify pipeline and measure time, then submit eval. If successful, scale up epochs later.
